step_by_step_thinking:
Answer:

We know that when a tooth is missing, adjacent teeth shift into the space. In this case, since there’s no permanent first molar (which normally sits mesially), the permanent second molar will move toward the mesial direction because it's next to it on the same dental arch. This movement happens due to lack of resistance from the missing tooth.

This movement happens due to lack of resistance from the missing tooth.

Answer: C: Mesial side
